brief_title: Lenvatinib in Combination With Pembrolizumab Versus Treatment of Physician's Choice in Participants With Advanced Endometrial Cancer (MK-3475-775/E7080-G000-309 Per Merck Standard Convention [KEYNOTE-775])
official_title: A Multicenter, Open-label, Randomized, Phase 3 Trial to Compare the Efficacy and Safety of Lenvatinib in Combination With Pembrolizumab Versus Treatment of Physician's Choice in Participants With Advanced Endometrial Cancer

pmids.citation: Marth C, Lorusso D, Bidzinski M, Scollo P, Vardar MA, Oaknin A, Kaen DL, Nogueira-Rodrigues A, Miller DS, Hasegawa K, MacKay H, Moore RG, Santin AD, Yunokawa M, Romero I, Pignata S, Baron-Hay S, Kim YM, Amit A, Ghamande S, Danska-Bidzinska A, Tarnawski R, Wang PH, Yu Z, Slomovitz BM, Okpara CE, McKenzie J, Meng R, Orlowski R, Makker V. outcomes.title: Progression Free Survival (PFS) Per Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors Version 1.1 (RECIST 1.1) Based on Blinded Independent Central Review (BICR) in Mismatch Repair Proficient (pMMR) Participants
outcomes.description: PFS was defined as the time from the date of randomization to the date of the first documentation of disease progression, as determined by Blinded Independent Central Review (BICR) per Response Evaluation Criteria In Solid Tumors (RECIST) version 1.1 or death due to any cause (whichever occurred fir

outcomes.title: PFS Per Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors Version 1.1 (RECIST 1.1) Based on BICR in All-comer Participants
outcomes.description: PFS was defined as the time from the date of randomization to the date of the first documentation of disease progression, as determined by Blinded Independent Central Review (BICR) per Response Evaluation Criteria In Solid Tumors (RECIST) version 1.1 or death due to any cause (whichever occurred fir

outcomes.title: Overall Survival (OS) in pMMR Participants
outcomes.description: OS was defined as the time from the date of randomization to the date of death due to any cause. Participants who were lost to follow-up and those who were alive at the date of data cut-off were censored at the date the participant was last known alive, or date of data cut-off, whichever occurred fi

outcomes.title: OS in All-comer Participants
outcomes.description: OS was defined as the time from the date of randomization to the date of death due to any cause. Participants who were lost to follow-up and those who were alive at the date of data cut-off were censored at the date the participant was last known alive, or date of data cut-off, whichever occurred fi

outcomes.title: Objective Response Rate (ORR) in pMMR Participants
outcomes.description: ORR was defined as the percentage of participants who had best overall response of either complete response (CR) or partial response (PR) as determined by BICR per RECIST 1.1. outcomes.title: Change From Baseline in European Organization for Research and Treatment of Cancer (EORTC) Quality of Life Questionnaire-Core 30 (QLQ-C30) in pMMR Participants
outcomes.description: EORTC QLQ-C30 was a questionnaire which included 30 questions that rates the overall quality of life in cancer participants. The first 28 questions use a 4-point scale (1=not at all to 4=very much) for evaluating function (physical, role, social, cognitive, emotional), symptoms (diarrhea, fatigue, d

outcomes.title: Number of Partricipants With Treatment-emergent Adverse Events (TEAEs), Serious Adverse Events (SAEs), and Immune-Related Adverse Events (irAEs)
outcomes.description: TEAEs were AEs that occurred (or worsened, if present at baseline) after the first dose of study drug through 28 days after the last dose. An AE was any untoward medical occurrence in a participant temporally associated with use of study treatment, whether or not related to the treatment. An SAE was

outcomes.title: Number of Participants Who Discontinued Study Treatment Due to a TEAE in pMMR Participants
outcomes.description: TEAEs was defined as those AEs that occurred (or worsened, if present at Baseline) after the first dose of study drug through 30 days after the last dose of study drug. An AE was defined as any untoward medical occurrence in a participants or clinical study participant temporally associated with the

outcomes.title: Number of Participants Who Discontinued Study Treatment Due to a TEAE in All-comer Participants
outcomes.description: TEAEs was defined as those AEs that occurred (or worsened, if present at Baseline) after the first dose of study drug through 30 days after the last dose of study drug. An AE was defined as any untoward medical occurrence in a participants or clinical study participant temporally associated with the

outcomes.title: Time to Treatment Failure Due to Toxicity in pMMR Participants
outcomes.description: Time to treatment failure due to toxicity was defined as the time from the date of randomization to the date a participant discontinued study treatment due to TEAEs.

outcomes.title: Time to Treatment Failure Due to Toxicity in All-comer Participants
outcomes.description: Time to treatment failure due to toxicity was defined as the time from the date of randomization to the date a participant discontinued study treatment due to TEAEs.

outcomes.title: Plasma Concentration of Lenvatinib Versus Time in All-comer Participants
outcomes.description: Pharmacokinetic (PK) samples were collected and analyzed using a population PK approach to estimate PK parameters. Individual predicted plasma concentration of lenvatinib was then derived from the PK model.

outcomes.title: Plasma Concentration of Lenvatinib Versus Time in pMMR Participants
outcomes.description: PK samples were collected and analyzed using a population PK approach to estimate PK parameters. Individual predicted plasma concentration of lenvatinib was then derived from the PK model.

outcomes.title: Model Predicted Apparent Total Clearance (CL/F) for Lenvatinib
outcomes.description: Sparse pharmacokinetic (PK) samples were collected and analyzed using a population PK approach to estimate PK parameters. Individual predicted CL/F for lenvatinib was then derived from the PK model. The data was collected and analyzed for lenvatinib plus pembrolizumab arm only.

outcomes.title: Model Predicted Area Under the Plasma Drug Concentration-time Curve (AUC) for Lenvatinib
outcomes.description: Sparse PK samples were collected and analyzed using a population PK approach to estimate PK parameters. Individual predicted AUC for lenvatinib was then derived from the PK model. The data was collected and analyzed for lenvatinib plus pembrolizumab arm only.
